Pass Criteria: Spare blocks correctly replace bad blocks

Persistence strategy, incremental persistence (after each Block Erase), periodic snapshot (every 10 minutes), graceful shutdown full persistence, dedicated background thread

Recovery mechanism, read persistence file on restart to restore DRAM state, read metadata files (l2p_table.bin, bad_block.bin, erase_count.bin) to restore FTL, CRC check failure logs warning and initializes to 0xFF, --fresh-start skips recovery

Storage partitions: Bootloader(4MB), Firmware Slot A(64MB), Firmware Slot B(64MB), Config Area(8MB), BBT(8MB), Log Area(16MB), System Info(4MB)
Operation commands: READ(0x03), FAST_READ(0x0B), PAGE_PROGRAM(0x02), SECTOR_ERASE(0x20), CHIP_ERASE(0xC7), WRITE_ENABLE(0x06), WRITE_DISABLE(0x04), READ_STATUS_REG(0x05), READ_ID(0x9F)
Data persistence, single binary file /var/hfsss/nor/firmware_storage.bin, each write operation synced to file (msync+fsync)

NAND driver API: nand_init/nand_deinit, nand_read_page_async/nand_write_page_async/nand_erase_block_async, nand_multi_plane_read_async/nand_multi_plane_write_async, nand_read_status/nand_read_id/nand_reset, nand_set_feature/nand_get_feature, nand_program_suspend/nand_program_resume/nand_erase_suspend, nand_get_die_state/nand_get_channel_earliest_available_time, nand_get_geometry/nand_get_timing

Driver internal implementation, maintain per-Channel command issue queue, send commands to media thread via message queue, maintain Die state machine (IDLE->CMD_RECEIVED->EXECUTING->COMPLETE), calculate simulated ONFI command sequences, track channel bus occupancy time

Driver internal implementation, NOR emulated as process shared memory byte array, read with direct memory access + delay injection (90ns*len), write checks write-enable + can only write 0 not 1, erase sets 0xFF + 200ms delay, write protection detection, persistence synced after each write/erase

Command completion submission, receive Application Layer command completion notification, build 64-byte CQE (DW0/DW1/DW2/DW3), call kernel module write_cqe_and_notify, kernel writes CQE to physical memory + delivers MSI-X interrupt

Async event management, maintain pending async event queue, Async Event Request command returns immediately or suspends, support Error Status/Smart/Health Status Change/Notice/NVM Command Set Specific events
PCIe link state management, monitor PCIe link state (Active/L0/L1/L2), implement ASPM software configuration, respond to PCIe hot reset/FLR to execute controller reset

Namespace management interface, maintain Namespace list (up to 4096), Namespace create/delete allocates/releases NAND space, update mapping tables, Namespace attributes (NSID/NSZE/NCAP/NUSE/feature flags)
NVMe power state emulation: PS0(Active 25W), PS1(Active reduced 18W), PS2(Idle 8W, 5ms enter/exit), PS3(Low Power 3W, 50ms/30ms), PS4(Deep Sleep 0.5W, 500ms/100ms)

Functional requirements: maintain current power state, respond to Set Feature (Power Management), record cumulative time per power state, automatic power state transition, simulate delay when exiting low power state, power consumption statistics interface

RTOS primitive implementation: Task simulated with pthread, support 1~32 priority levels, task states (Ready/Running/Blocked/Suspended), time slice default 1ms, task stack default 16KB, task_create/task_delete/task_suspend/task_resume API
Message queue: fixed-size/variable-length messages, blocking/non-blocking send/receive, timeout parameter, queue depth default 64, thread-safe implementation

Semaphore/Mutex/Event group: binary/counting semaphore, sem_binary_create/sem_take/sem_give, sem_counting_create, timeout support, priority inheritance to prevent priority inversion, recursive mutex, 32-bit event flags event_set/event_clear/event_wait

Software timer/Memory pool: high-resolution clock based (CLOCK_MONOTONIC), one-shot/periodic trigger, precision 100us, timer callback executes in Timer task, fixed-size block memory pool (32B/64B/128B/256B/512B/1KB/4KB), pool_alloc/pool_free

Static task binding: critical tasks bound to dedicated CPU cores, FTL->Core 0-3, GC/WL->Core 4-5, HAL NAND Driver->Core 6-21, NVMe Interface->Core 22-23, Common Service->Core 24-25, implemented via pthread_setaffinity_np

Priority scheduling/Load balancing: priority-based preemptive scheduling, SCHED_FIFO real-time policy, high priority tasks can preempt low priority, FTL task dynamic assignment, monitor each firmware core CPU usage (>85% triggers load balancing), scheduling statistics (runtime/wait time/preemption count)
Memory partition planning: firmware working memory total 4GB, FTL mapping table 2GB, command buffer 512MB, Write Buffer 1GB, system heap 256MB, kernel code/data 128MB, reserved/protected 128MB

Memory management strategy: large data structures statically pre-allocated at startup, mmap(MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_POPULATE)+mlock, small objects use dedicated memory pool, avoid malloc/free on critical path, mprotect access protection for critical data regions, Guard Page for stack overflow detection, monitor available memory below threshold triggers pressure alert
Boot sequence: Phase 0 Power-on reset, Phase 1 Bootloader execution, Phase 2 Firmware image loading, Phase 3 Hardware initialization, Phase 4 FTL initialization, Phase 5 System ready
Bootloader features: boot time simulation 3-8 seconds, dual-image redundancy Slot A/B switching, secure boot SHA-256 verification, boot log written to NOR Log partition

Power-on service: detect last power-off type (normal/abnormal/power-cut), normal shutdown restores from persistence, abnormal shutdown checks Write Buffer log replay or discard, POST self-test, SMART records power-on count

Power-off service: normal shutdown (NVMe Shutdown) stops new IO, waits for in-flight IO completion, flushes Write Buffer, updates L2P/BBT/PE counts, writes integrity flag, sets CSTS.SHST=0x02; abnormal shutdown atexit/signal handler catches SIGTERM, performs best-effort rapid persistence, maintains WAL, SMART records unsafe shutdown count
Pass Criteria: Data fully saved on normal shutdown

Description: Interface form: Unix Domain Socket (/var/run/hfsss.sock) JSON-RPC, /proc filesystem interface (/proc/hfsss/), REST API (optional V2.0 localhost:8080)

OOB management functions: status query GET /status, SMART query GET /smart, performance statistics GET /perf, parameter modification POST /config, fault injection POST /fault, force GC POST /gc/trigger, snapshot save POST /snapshot, log export GET /log, channel statistics GET /channel/{id}, Die status GET /die/{ch}/{chip}/{die}

SMART information implementation: Log Page ID=0x02, Critical Warning(0), Temperature(1-2), Available Spare(3), Available Spare Threshold(4), Percentage Used(5), Data Units Read(32-47), Data Units Written(48-63), Host Read Commands(64-79), Host Write Commands(80-95), Controller Busy Time(96-111), Power Cycles(112-127), Power On Hours(128-143), Unsafe Shutdowns(144-159), Media and Data Integrity Errors(160-175)

Communication mechanism: message passing (per core pair SPSC Ring Buffer), message format (type 4B + length 4B + max 256B), ipc_send/ipc_recv API, eventfd notification; shared memory (large data pass pointers), reference counting, access protection (RWLock/seqlock); inter-core signals (ipc_signal simulates SGI); inter-core spinlock (GCC atomics, timeout-based deadlock detection)

Watchdog: each task feeds watchdog every 500ms (watchdog_feed), Watchdog periodically checks all task feed status, timeout without feeding treated as hung triggering system Panic, Watchdog timeout configurable per task type

System resource monitoring: CPU usage per-second statistics for each firmware core thread, memory usage monitoring per memory partition, NAND channel queue depth monitoring, free block watermark monitoring per Die

Performance anomaly detection/Temperature emulation: command latency P50/P90/P99/P99.9 real-time calculation, P99.9 exceeding threshold logs alert and ASYNC EVENT notification, GC efficiency monitoring, T=T_ambient+IOPS*COEFF_I+BW*COEFF_B temperature estimation, temperature >70C sets SMART Critical Warning, temperature >75C throttles (IOPS limit 50%)

Assert mechanism: ASSERT(condition, msg) macro, false condition triggers assertion failure, prints filename/line number/condition/error message, logs to log, performs scene preservation, calls Panic procedure

Panic procedure: set global Panic flag (atomic operation), stop accepting new NVMe commands, save all firmware core runtime state (register snapshot/stack backtrace), write critical state (L2P table summary/error context) to Panic Dump (/var/hfsss/panic_dump_<timestamp>.bin), deliver Controller Fatal Status to host (CSTS.CFS=1), perform best-effort data persistence, record Panic reason to NOR Log, exit daemon, configure ulimit -c unlimited to generate coredump

Debug functions: Command Trace with full trace enabled (records each command receive/process/complete time/operation sequence), Trace ring buffer default keeps last 100000 entries, export JSON Lines format, dynamically controlled via OOB interface; NAND operation Trace records each NAND command detail, filterable by Channel; FTL operation Trace records L2P lookup/GC trigger/WL migration, output compatible with FlashSim/MQSim; daemon supports GDB attach, provides Python GDB scripts for key data structures, supports GDB breakpoints; fine-grained performance counters by command type/LBA range/Channel for IOPS/bandwidth/latency stats, exported via /proc/hfsss/perf_counters or OOB, supports counter Reset
Event levels: FATAL(0), ERROR(1), WARN(2), INFO(3), DEBUG(4), TRACE(5)

Log storage: memory Ring Buffer default 64MB, WARN and above written to NOR Log partition, DEBUG and above written to /var/log/hfsss/hfsss.log, supports logrotate, WARN and above synced to Linux syslog (journald); Log entry format: [timestamp us][level][module name][Core ID] message content {structured fields JSON}

Address mapping architecture: default page-level mapping (Page-Level Mapping, 4KB/entry), PPN 64bit encoding ([63:48]Reserved, [47:40]Channel ID, [39:36]Chip ID, [35:34]Die ID, [33:32]Plane ID, [31:19]Block ID, [18:10]Page ID, [9:0]Offset)

Mapping table design: full L2P mapping table stored in DRAM, 2TB SSD (512M pages) each mapping 4 bytes total 2GB, mmap(MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_HUGETLB) allocates 2MB huge pages, invalid LPN maps to 0xFFFFFFFF (INVALID_PPN); reverse mapping table (P2L) info stored in per-page OOB area (LPN + write timestamp); in-memory sparse P2L cache (GC candidate Blocks only); L2P mapping table Checkpoint persistence (full write to file every 1GB of writes), incremental persistence (write incremental log WAL after each GC completion), load latest Checkpoint on power-up and replay WAL to recover complete mapping table

Over-provisioning: reserve 20% NAND space as OP area (not mapped to any LBA, dedicated to GC), OP_Blocks=Total_Blocks*0.20, user can adjust OP ratio (7%-50%) via Format NVM, higher OP improves GC efficiency and lowers WAF

Write operation flow: Data DMA from host memory to Write Buffer, for each 4KB logical page lookup old PPN, if valid mark old PPN as Invalid (increment that Block's invalid page count), allocate new free physical page (from Current Write Block), update L2P[LPN]=new_ppn, write Data to corresponding NAND media (async), write LPN in OOB area, when Current Write Block is full close and add to Full Block list, open new Write Block, return NVMe command completion

Read operation flow: for each 4KB logical page query L2P table ppn=L2P[LPN], if ppn==INVALID_PPN return all-zero data or Error, parse ppn to physical address (ch/chip/die/plane/block/page), async read via HAL NAND driver, ECC check on read completion (correct if correctable, report Error if uncorrectable), Data DMA to host memory, return NVMe command completion

Striping strategy: consecutive logical pages assigned Round-Robin to different Channel Write Blocks, stripe size default 1 NAND page (16KB), alignment optimization 1MB write each 16KB to one Channel (16 Channels perfectly aligned), intra-Channel further cross-Plane striping enables Multi-Plane Program

Block state machine: FREE->[allocated as Write Block]->OPEN->[full]->FULL->[selected for GC]->VICTIM->ERASING->[GC reclaimed and erased]->FREE; Block metadata (erase_count, valid_page_count, invalid_page_count, free_page_count, last_erase_time, first_write_time, read_count, state)

Current Write Block management: each Die/Plane maintains one Current Write Block, new writes sequentially written, full block closed and new CWB opened, multi-Plane mode simultaneously maintains two Plane CWBs on same Die (same Block offset), supports Multi-Plane Program, Plane-level CWB ensures Block/Page alignment constraints for each multi-plane write

Free block pool management: global free block pool organized hierarchically by Channel/Die/Plane as free block linked lists, allocation strategy prioritizes least-worn blocks (lowest erase_count), block shortage protection triggers emergency GC when any Die free block count < LOW_WATERMARK (default 5 blocks) and pauses new write allocation

GC trigger strategy: high watermark (global free block ratio <20%) starts background GC (low priority, no impact on foreground IO), low watermark (global free block ratio <5%) starts emergency GC (high priority, throttles foreground writes), critically low watermark (global free block ratio <2%) stops all write commands and focuses on GC reclamation, Per-Die GC each Die independently maintains watermarks allowing local GC

Victim Block selection algorithm: Greedy algorithm (default) selects Block with fewest valid pages as Victim, maximizes free pages reclaimed per GC cycle, minimizes valid page migration, maintains sorted set (min-heap) by valid_page_count per Die, time complexity O(log n); Cost-Benefit algorithm considers block utilization and age (time since last erase), Cost-Benefit=(1-utilization)/(2*utilization)*age, prefers high Cost-Benefit Blocks (high invalidity + old data), helps reduce cold data interference with hot data regions; FIFO algorithm selects oldest Full Block by close time; Hot-Cold separation classifies written data by frequency into Hot (frequently updated) and Cold (infrequently updated), Hot data written to dedicated Hot Blocks, Cold data to Cold Blocks, GC preferentially reclaims Hot area Blocks (low valid ratio), Cold area GC triggers infrequently reducing cold data migration

GC execution flow: select Victim Block, scan all pages in Victim Block, for each page read OOB-stored LPN, query L2P[page_lpn] to verify if current PPN matches (page still valid), if valid (L2P[page_lpn]==current_ppn) read out page data, allocate new free page, write data to new page, update L2P[page_lpn]=new_ppn, increment old_ppn Block's invalid_page_count; if invalid skip; after all valid pages migrated erase Victim Block, update Block metadata (erase_count++, state=FREE), add erased Block to free block pool

GC concurrency optimization/Write amplification analysis: multi-Die parallel GC can run simultaneously on multiple Dies without interference; Pipeline GC reads Victim Block valid pages (Read Phase) and writes to new Block (Write Phase) can pipeline across different Dies; GC pause and resume when high-priority IO arrives suspend current NAND Program (Program Suspend) to prioritize IO then resume GC (Program Resume); WAF=NAND_Writes/Host_Writes, ideal WAF~=1/(1-utilization_of_data), target WAF<=3 at 90% drive occupancy steady state (TLC NAND, Greedy GC, 20% OP); real-time WAF statistics exported via OOB; reduce WAF through hot-cold separation, larger OP ratio, optimized GC strategy

Dynamic wear leveling: during GC, prefer writing data to free Blocks with lower P/E cycle count (consider erase_count during free block allocation), implementation maintains min-heap of free blocks sorted by erase_count, allocation always picks block with lowest erase_count, effect: Blocks frequently reclaimed by GC in hot data regions gradually replaced by younger blocks

Static wear leveling: periodically check global Block erase_count distribution (using erase_count histogram), when some Blocks' erase_count significantly below average (default threshold: below 50% of average) trigger static WL; Static WL flow: select cold data Block with lowest erase_count (victim_cold_block), select hot area free Block with highest erase_count (donor_hot_block from frequently erased GC-reclaimed blocks), migrate cold data from victim_cold_block to donor_hot_block, erase victim_cold_block and allocate as new free block for hot data region use; through this swap low-PE blocks rotate to hot data area, high-PE blocks used for cold data; trigger frequency: default once every 100 dynamic GC cycles (configurable)

Wear monitoring and alerting: real-time statistics of whole-SSD average P/E cycle count and max P/E cycle count, when max P/E count approaches NAND lifetime limit (>80% of PE_CYCLE_LIMIT) set SMART Available Spare low alert, when Available Spare < Available Spare Threshold set SMART Critical Warning (bit 0), dynamically recommend OP ratio adjustment based on wear state (higher wear suggests increasing OP)
Command state machine: NVMe Command arrives->[RECEIVED]->[PARSING]->[L2P_LOOKUP]->[NAND_QUEUED]->[NAND_EXECUTING]->[ECC_CHECK]->[ERROR?]->No->[COMPLETE], Yes->[RETRY/FAIL]

Read Retry mechanism: when NAND Read produces correctable ECC error first attempt soft decode (Soft-Decision LDPC), if soft decode fails trigger Read Retry adjusting NAND read voltage (Vread) offset and re-read, Read Retry up to 15 attempts (default, per NAND die spec), each attempt adjusts different Voltage Offset; after 15 retries still failing mark as uncorrectable error (UCE), return NVMe error completion (Media and Data Integrity Error), UCE recorded in NVMe Error Log Page, increment SMART Media and Data Integrity Errors count

Write Retry/Write Verify: on Program failure (status register WR_FAIL bit set) attempt writing data to spare area in same Block or new Block, failure count exceeding threshold (default 3) marks Block as bad and removes from FTL, replaced with new block from free pool; Write Verify optionally reads back data after program for verification (Write-and-Verify mode impacts write performance), disabled by default configurable for high-reliability scenarios

Multi-level flow control: Host IO rate limiting via Token Bucket, per-Namespace and global two-level rate limiting, IOPS limit 1K-2000K (100 IOPS granularity), bandwidth limit 50MB/s-14GB/s (10MB/s granularity), rate-exceeded commands enter wait queue (max wait queue depth = SQ depth); GC/WL bandwidth quota controls GC NAND bandwidth (default max 30%, min 5%), GC can increase to higher quota (max 80%) when host IO load is low, adaptive adjustment samples host IO and GC bandwidth per second, dynamically adjusts GC quota based on current free block watermark; NAND channel-level flow control per-Channel command queue depth limit (default 128), queue full causes upper command dispatcher to wait (back-pressure propagation), channel starvation detection checks if waiting commands are forgotten; Write buffer flow control based on Write Buffer occupancy ratio triggers 3-level flow control (<60% normal unrestricted, 60%-80% light throttle host write rate reduced 20%, 80%-90% heavy throttle host write rate reduced 50%, >90% suspend new write commands, resume when buffer drains below 70%)

RAID-Like data protection: LDPC ECC (first line of defense) each NAND page with ECC parity in OOB area, LDPC encoding with BCH as backup, LDPC as primary (corrects 16-24bit/KB), soft decode retains multi-read probability info for Soft-Decision improving correction capability, ECC computed by dedicated firmware thread (simulates hardware ECC engine behavior); Cross-Die parity (Die-Level Parity) (optional V2.0) computes XOR parity across multi-Die writes, parity page written to independent Parity Die, when any Die has whole-block uncorrectable error data recoverable via parity, implements simple RAID-5 logic (for multi-Die scenarios); Critical metadata redundancy: L2P mapping table dual-stored (DRAM + persistence file), BBT stored as primary and backup copies in NOR Flash, P/E count table also dual-protected, periodic consistency check between two metadata copies with alert on mismatch; Write Buffer power-loss protection simulates SSD controller supercapacitor protection mechanism via WAL: each Write Buffer write operation first writes to WAL file (synchronous), then updates DRAM data, on power loss recovery scans WAL and replays ensuring data consistency, WAL file stored on host high-speed NVMe (e.g. another real NVMe SSD) ensuring WAL write speed is not a bottleneck

NVMe error status codes/Error handling flow: supports Generic Status Code Type(0x00) Success(0x00), Invalid Command Opcode(0x01), Invalid Field in Command(0x02), Invalid Namespace or Format(0x06), Invalid I/O Queue Identifier(0x0A), Maximum Queue Size Exceeded(0x0B), LBA Out of Range(0x80), Capacity Exceeded(0x81), Namespace Not Ready(0x82); Command Specific Status Code Type(0x01) Completion Queue Invalid(0x00), Invalid Queue Identifier(0x01); Media & Data Integrity Status Code Type(0x02) Write Fault(0x80), Unrecovered Read Error(0x81), End-to-End Guard Check Error(0x82), Compare Failure(0x85)

Recoverable error retry (internal retry transparent to host), exceeding retry count degrades to unrecoverable error, recorded in Error Log Page (internal only); Unrecoverable data error returns corresponding NVMe error status code to host (DNR=0 allows host retry), marks LBA range as "error reported", writes to NVMe Error Log Page (Sequence Number, Error Count, CID, SQ ID, Error Location, LBA, NS, Type), SMART increments Media and Data Integrity Errors count; NAND device error: bad block handling marks bad block, replaces with spare block, migrates valid data; NAND entire Chip failure (extreme scenario) attempts recovery via cross-Die parity if available, otherwise reports read error for corresponding LBA range to host, triggers AER notifying host Critical Warning (bit 2: Media Related); Command timeout handling returns Abort command completion to host (if timed-out command internally aborted), if command timeout unrecoverable triggers Controller Reset; Firmware internal error: ASSERT failure/NULL pointer access etc. triggers Panic procedure, non-critical errors (e.g. memory pool exhaustion) recorded in Error Log and continues in degraded mode (DEGRADED)

Random read IOPS (4KB, QD=32): single namespace no ECC target 1000000 IOPS, single namespace with ECC target 600000 IOPS, multi-namespace (4) total 800000 IOPS, steady-state (90% drive occupancy) target 400000 IOPS
Random write IOPS (4KB, QD=32): initial state (Fresh Out Of Box) target 300000 IOPS, steady-state (90% occupancy, WAF~2) target 150000 IOPS, steady-state (50% occupancy, WAF~1.5) target 200000 IOPS
Mixed read/write IOPS (4KB, 70R/30W, QD=32): steady-state target 250000 IOPS
Sequential read 128KB target 6.5GB/s, sequential write 128KB target 3.5GB/s, sequential read 1MB target 7.0GB/s, sequential write 1MB target 4.0GB/s, random read 4KB (QD=128) target 4.0GB/s

Random read (QD=1) P50 80us, P99 150us, P99.9 300us (includes NAND read latency model TLC LSB:35us + transfer); Random write (QD=1) P50 20us, P99 50us, P99.9 100us (Write Buffer hit, excludes NAND program latency); Forced read (FUA write + read) 1ms/3ms/8ms (includes NAND Program latency TLC:800us); GC pause read 200us/500us/2ms (read command latency during GC execution); Flush command 500ms/2s/5s (depends on Write Buffer backlog)

NAND Page Read latency error <5%, NAND Page Program latency error <5%, NAND Block Erase latency error <5%, overall IOPS simulation accuracy <10%, WAF simulation accuracy <15%, GC trigger timing accuracy <1% watermark deviation

Max supported Channels 32, max supported Namespaces 4096, max NAND capacity 256TB (simulated, limited by DRAM size), max concurrent NVMe queues 4096 SQ/CQ pairs, max concurrent commands 65535 (NVMe spec limit), CPU core scalability from 64 to 256 cores linear scaling, IOPS increases with core count

NAND media thread CPU 70-90% (full load) (16 Channels * 2-3 threads overall utilization), FTL thread CPU 60-80% (full load) (4-8 FTL cores), PCIe/NVMe module CPU 40-60% (full load) (IO Dispatcher + Worker), DRAM utilization >85% (simulation storage + mapping table + cache), host OS CPU usage <10% (does not impact other host services)

Block device node /dev/nvme<n>n<m>, device type NVMe Namespace (Block Device), block size 4096 bytes (Logical Block Size=4KB), namespace capacity configurable default 2TB, interface protocol NVMe 2.0, transport protocol PCIe (virtual)

nvme list: list simulated NVMe devices, nvme id-ctrl: query controller identity, nvme id-ns: query namespace attributes, nvme smart-log: read SMART info, nvme error-log: read error log, nvme get-feature: query features, nvme set-feature: set features, nvme format: format, nvme fw-download: firmware download, nvme fw-commit: firmware commit, nvme create-ns: create namespace, nvme delete-ns: delete namespace, nvme attach-ns: attach namespace
Support ioengine=io_uring, support direct=1 (O_DIRECT), support numjobs=32 multi-threaded, support iodepth=128 deep queue, fio test runs normally

Unix Domain Socket path /var/run/hfsss/hfsss.sock, protocol JSON-RPC 2.0, GET /status returns simulator overall status, GET /nand/channel/{ch_id} queries Channel status, POST /fault/inject fault injection, POST /config/gc modifies GC configuration

/proc/hfsss/status simulator status (read-only), /proc/hfsss/config current configuration (read-only), /proc/hfsss/perf_counters performance counters (read-only), /proc/hfsss/channel_stats per-Channel statistics (read-only), /proc/hfsss/ftl_stats FTL statistics (mapping table hit rate, GC count, etc.), /proc/hfsss/latency_hist latency histogram (read-only), /proc/hfsss/version simulator version info

hfsss-ctrl status: view status, hfsss-ctrl perf: view performance statistics (real-time refresh), hfsss-ctrl channel 3: view Channel 3 details, hfsss-ctrl fault inject --type=bad_block --ch=3 --chip=1 --die=0 --block=2048: fault injection, hfsss-ctrl config set gc.algorithm=greedy: configuration change, hfsss-ctrl gc trigger: manual GC trigger, hfsss-ctrl snapshot save: force persistence, hfsss-ctrl log dump --level=warn --last=1000: export last 1000 WARN+ logs, hfsss-ctrl trace start: start command Trace, hfsss-ctrl trace stop: stop command Trace, hfsss-ctrl trace dump --output=/tmp/trace.json: export Trace data
YAML format configuration file /etc/hfsss/hfsss.yaml, configuration items: version, system, memory, cpu, nvme, nand, nor, ftl, persistence, oob, debug

NAND data file format: each Plane data file stored independently, file header contains Magic Number/Format Version/File Creation Time/Last Modified Time/Channel ID/Chip ID/Die ID/Plane ID/Blocks Per Plane/Pages Per Block/Page Size/Total File Size/CRC32, data area linearly arranged by Block*Page; L2P mapping table file format: Header contains Magic/Version/Total LPN Count/Checkpoint Sequence Number/Timestamp/CRC64, continuous uint64_t array index=LPN value=PPN; WAL format: each WAL record 64 bytes containing Sequence Number/Record Type/Payload Length/Payload/CRC32, supports L2P Update/Block State Change/Erase Count Update record types

Bad Block injection parameters: ch/chip/die/plane/block; Read Error injection parameters: ch/chip/die/plane/block/page; Program Error injection parameters: ch/chip/die/plane/block/page; Erase Error injection parameters: ch/chip/die/plane/block; Bit Flip injection parameters: ch/chip/die/plane/block/page/bit_pos; Read Disturb Storm injection parameters: ch/chip/die/block; Data Retention injection parameters: ch/chip/die/plane/block/aging_factor

Sudden Power Off: immediately terminate daemon (simulate power loss) without performing persistence; Power Off During Write: simulate power loss during Write Buffer flush to NAND; Power Off During GC: simulate power loss during GC execution

Memory Corruption: inject random errors in L2P mapping table; Firmware Panic: trigger firmware Panic to test Panic handling flow; Channel Timeout: make all operations on specified Channel timeout; NVMe Queue Corruption: inject CQE errors (Phase Tag flip, etc.)

Execute fault injection via OOB interface (POST /fault/inject) or hfsss-ctrl fault inject CLI tool; supports immediate injection (immediate=true) triggers on next operation; delayed injection (delay_ms=N) triggers after N milliseconds; probabilistic injection (probability=0.01) triggers with specified probability per operation; persistent injection (persistent=true) stays active until explicitly cleared; one-shot injection (persistent=false, default) auto-clears after single trigger

Under normal IO workload (fio mixed read/write, 64 threads, QD=128) MTBF >= 720 hours (30 days continuous operation); under fault injection testing able to correctly handle all defined fault types without Panic or data corruption

Under normal operation (no fault injection) read-back data 100% consistent with written data (md5sum verification); after normal shutdown (NVMe Shutdown command) and restart all completed write commands (FUA=1 or after Flush) data must be fully recovered; after simulated power loss (SIGKILL) and restart may lose Write Buffer data not yet persisted, but persisted data 100% intact

Long-running stability: no crash during 72-hour sustained IO stress test; memory leak: memory growth <1MB/hour; correctly handles extreme scenarios: NAND capacity full (no free blocks), all spare blocks exhausted, etc. should return correct error codes rather than crash; concurrency safety: no data races during multi-threaded concurrent access to shared data structures (verified via Thread Sanitizer)
Simulate supercapacitor with configurable capacitance (1-10F), ESR, and voltage range; calculate drain time and energy budget for power-loss protection
Test Requirements: Measure drain time vs. calculated value under varying load profiles
Pass Criteria: Drain time within 5% of analytical model

Description: Implement Normal->PowerFail->CapDrain->SafeState transitions with <1us power-fail detection latency
Test Requirements: Inject power-fail event and measure state machine transition times

Description: Guarantee 4KB aligned writes are power-safe; detect torn writes via sequence number + CRC validation
Metrics: Zero torn writes for atomic-sized IOs
Test Requirements: Inject power-fail at random points during 4KB writes and verify integrity on recovery
Pass Criteria: No torn writes detected across 10000 injection cycles

Description: Flush L2P journal entries to NAND within supercapacitor drain window to ensure metadata consistency after UPLP
Test Requirements: Inject power-fail and verify L2P mapping consistency after recovery
Pass Criteria: L2P table matches pre-power-fail committed state

Description: Prioritized flush during capacitor drain: L2P -> BBT -> SMART -> WAL -> SysInfo, ordered by criticality
Metrics: All critical metadata flushed within energy budget
Test Requirements: Verify flush order and completeness under minimum drain time configuration
Pass Criteria: All critical metadata persisted in correct priority order

Description: Detect unclean shutdown, replay WAL, rebuild FTL state; target recovery time <5s for 1TB capacity
Test Requirements: Simulate unclean shutdown on 1TB namespace and measure recovery time
Pass Criteria: Recovery completes in <5s with consistent state

Description: Injectable power-fail with configurable drain time and injection point for testing UPLP at any FTL phase
Metrics: Fault injection at any FTL phase
Test Requirements: Inject power-fail at each FTL processing phase and verify recovery
Pass Criteria: System recovers correctly from injection at every phase

Description: SMART field (Unsafe Shutdowns) accurately tracks UPLP events across power cycles
Test Requirements: Perform N UPLP events and verify SMART counter equals N
Pass Criteria: Counter value equals actual UPLP event count

Description: Deficit Weighted Round-Robin scheduler with deficit counters and configurable per-namespace weights for bandwidth-proportional scheduling
Test Requirements: Run concurrent IO on multiple namespaces with different weights and measure bandwidth distribution
Pass Criteria: Measured bandwidth ratio within +/-5% of configured weight ratio

Description: Token bucket IOPS enforcement with 1K-2M IOPS configurable granularity per namespace
Test Requirements: Set IOPS limit per namespace and measure actual IOPS under saturation
Pass Criteria: Measured IOPS within +/-3% of configured limit

Description: Token bucket bandwidth enforcement with 50MB/s-14GB/s configurable range per namespace
Test Requirements: Set bandwidth limit per namespace and measure actual throughput under saturation
Pass Criteria: Measured bandwidth within +/-5% of configured limit

Description: Per-namespace P99 latency target monitoring with violation alerts via async event notification
Test Requirements: Configure P99 latency SLA and inject latency-inducing load to trigger violation
Pass Criteria: Alert generated within 10s of sustained SLA violation

Description: Change QoS parameters (weights/IOPS limits/BW limits) via OOB interface without service restart
Test Requirements: Change QoS policy via OOB and measure time to new policy taking effect
Pass Criteria: New policy enforced within 100ms of configuration change

Description: Background tasks (GC/WL) yield to host IO when host P99 latency exceeds 2x baseline to prevent GC-induced latency spikes
Metrics: Host P99 latency <2x baseline during GC
Test Requirements: Run host IO workload during active GC and measure P99 latency
Pass Criteria: P99 latency remains below 2x baseline throughout GC

Description: Configurable duty cycle for HOST_IO, GC_ALLOWED, and GC_ONLY time windows to provide deterministic latency behavior
Test Requirements: Configure deterministic windows and measure actual window boundaries

Module: T10 DIF/PI (Data Integrity Field / Protection Information)
Description: Configurable Protection Information type (Type 1/Type 2/Type 3) per namespace with correct guard/reference/application tag generation
Metrics: Correct PI generation per PI type
Test Requirements: Create namespaces with each PI type and verify tag generation on writes
Pass Criteria: Tags generated correctly per NVMe/T10 PI specification for each type

Module: T10 DIF/PI (Data Integrity Field / Protection Information)
Description: Compute and verify CRC-16 guard tag on write path (generate) and read path (verify) for end-to-end data integrity
Test Requirements: Inject single-bit errors in data and verify guard tag check detects all
Pass Criteria: All single-bit data corruption detected by CRC-16 guard tag

Module: T10 DIF/PI (Data Integrity Field / Protection Information)
Description: Process reference tag (LBA-based for Type 1/2) and application tag per NVMe specification for each PI type
Metrics: Correct tag matching per PI type
Test Requirements: Write data with PI and verify reference/application tags on readback
Pass Criteria: Tags match expected values per PI type specification

Module: T10 DIF/PI (Data Integrity Field / Protection Information)
Description: Preserve Protection Information metadata through entire FTL pipeline including GC valid page migration, ensuring PI is not lost or corrupted during internal data movement
Test Requirements: Write data with PI then trigger GC on those blocks and verify PI on readback
Pass Criteria: PI metadata intact after GC migration

Module: T10 DIF/PI (Data Integrity Field / Protection Information)
Description: Return correct NVMe status codes for guard tag failure (End-to-End Guard Check Error), reference tag failure (End-to-End Reference Tag Check Error), and application tag failure (End-to-End Application Tag Check Error)
Metrics: Correct NVMe error status returned for each tag failure type
Test Requirements: Inject guard/reference/application tag mismatches and verify error status codes
Pass Criteria: Correct NVMe status code returned for each PI error type

Description: Simulate AES-XTS 256-bit encryption/decryption on data path using XOR-based placeholder matching AES-XTS interface for functional verification of encryption pipeline
Test Requirements: Write data through encryption path and read back through decryption path
Pass Criteria: Data identical after encrypt-decrypt round trip

Description: Implement 3-level key hierarchy: Master Key (MK) -> Key Encryption Key (KEK) -> Data Encryption Key (DEK), with per-namespace DEK isolation
Metrics: Correct key derivation and wrapping at each level
Test Requirements: Verify key derivation chain and per-namespace DEK isolation
Pass Criteria: Each namespace uses unique DEK derived through correct hierarchy

Description: Implement TCG Opal SSC basic command set: StartSession, Authenticate, Set/Get locking state for per-locking-range access control
Test Requirements: Execute lock/unlock sequences on different locking ranges and verify access control
Pass Criteria: Data inaccessible when locked and accessible when unlocked per range

Description: Destroy namespace DEK rendering all data on that namespace cryptographically unreadable without physical erase
Metrics: Post-erase reads return error or garbage data
Test Requirements: Perform crypto erase on namespace and attempt to read previously written data
Pass Criteria: Reads return error or non-original data after crypto erase

Description: Block erase all user data blocks for target namespace ensuring no recoverable user data remains
Test Requirements: Perform secure erase and verify all blocks in namespace are erased (0xFF)
Pass Criteria: No user data recoverable after secure erase

Description: Simulate ROM->Bootloader->Firmware verification chain using CRC-based signatures; abort boot on tampered image detection
Test Requirements: Tamper firmware image and verify boot process aborts with error
Pass Criteria: Boot halts and reports integrity error on tampered image

Description: Store encryption keys in dual-copy key partition in NOR Flash with power-safe atomic updates to survive UPLP events
Test Requirements: Perform UPLP during key update and verify key integrity after recovery
Pass Criteria: Both key copies consistent and valid after UPLP

Description: Allocate capacity from global free pool, initialize per-namespace FTL tables (L2P, block lists), and make namespace usable after creation
Test Requirements: Create namespace via nvme create-ns and perform IO
Pass Criteria: IO succeeds on newly created namespace

Description: Reclaim all blocks belonging to namespace, free per-namespace L2P tables and metadata, return capacity to global free pool
Test Requirements: Delete namespace and verify free pool capacity increases by deleted namespace size
Pass Criteria: Free pool capacity equals pre-create value after delete

Description: Attach namespace to controller making it accessible, detach without data loss preserving all namespace data and metadata
Test Requirements: Write data then detach/attach namespace and verify data integrity
Pass Criteria: All data readable and correct after detach/attach cycle

Description: Maintain separate L2P mapping table per namespace with configurable shared or dedicated block pools ensuring namespace isolation
Metrics: NS isolation: write to NS-A does not affect NS-B mapping
Test Requirements: Write to one namespace and verify other namespace L2P mappings unchanged

Description: Per-namespace LBA format change (e.g. 512B to 4KB block size) without affecting other namespaces
Metrics: Format completes without affecting other namespaces
Test Requirements: Format one namespace while running IO on another and verify both
Pass Criteria: Formatted namespace uses new LBA size; other namespace unaffected

Description: Calculate weighted average composite temperature from per-die thermal sensor readings using configurable weight factors
Metrics: Composite temperature accuracy +/-2C vs thermal model
Test Requirements: Compare composite temperature calculation against reference thermal model
Pass Criteria: Composite temperature within +/-2C of reference model

Description: Implement 3-level progressive thermal throttling: Warning (75C, 80% performance), Throttle (80C, 50% performance), Critical (85C, 20% performance)
Metrics: IOPS reduction within +/-5% of target at each level
Test Requirements: Drive temperature through each threshold and measure IOPS reduction
Pass Criteria: IOPS reduced to target percentage +/-5% at each thermal level

Description: Controller-initiated graceful shutdown at 90C threshold to prevent thermal damage with safe data persistence
Test Requirements: Drive temperature to 90C and measure time to shutdown initiation

Description: Support NVMe Telemetry Host-Initiated Log Page (07h) with internal state dump including FTL statistics and NAND health data
Metrics: Log page populated on host request
Test Requirements: Issue Get Log Page 07h and verify contents
Pass Criteria: Log page contains valid internal state data

Description: Support NVMe Telemetry Controller-Initiated Log Page (08h) with autonomous event capture, generating AER notification to host
Metrics: AER notification sent on controller-initiated telemetry event
Test Requirements: Trigger internal event that generates controller-initiated telemetry and verify AER
Pass Criteria: AER received by host with correct telemetry log page reference

Description: Configurable vendor-specific log page with internal counters (GC count/WAF/channel utilization/error rates) accessible via NVMe Get Log Page
Metrics: Log page accessible via NVMe Get Log Page command
Test Requirements: Issue Get Log Page for vendor-specific log ID and verify counter values
Pass Criteria: Log page returns valid and accurate internal counter data

Description: Predict remaining endurance (percentage life remaining) based on current PE cycle consumption and WAF trend extrapolation
Metrics: Prediction within +/-10% of actual remaining life
Test Requirements: Compare predicted remaining life against actual PE cycle headroom

Description: Generate Asynchronous Event Request (AER) notifications for temperature threshold crossing, spare capacity below threshold, and reliability degradation events
Metrics: AER delivered within 1ms of triggering event
Test Requirements: Trigger each AER event type and measure notification latency
Pass Criteria: AER delivered to host within 1ms for all event types
